title: Valuation Surveyors (All levels)
description: **Who Are We** Founded in 1896, Knight Frank was registered as Knight Frank LLP a Limited Liability Partnership, on 3 November 2003, registered in England \& Wales at 55 Baker Street, London, W1U 8AN with the registered number of OC305934\. Headquartered in London, UK, Knight Frank is one of the world's leading independent real estate consultancies. We work responsibly in partnership to enhance people's lives and environments ***in over 50 Markets, 600\+ Offices, 20,000\+ People, 1 Global Network.*** At the heart of all we do are our Clients. A relationship built on trust is vital. As trusted partners in property, we act with integrity and care, understanding our clients’ unique needs and consistently thinking about the bigger picture to provide personalised, clear and considered advice across all areas of property. Through our deep understanding of key markets and sectors – both emerging and established – we are dedicated to meeting and exceeding their property goals. A true partnership is a balanced one, and our People are passionate about, and committed to, making our business inclusive and diverse. We give every individual the space and opportunity to perform at their best and be recognised for the immense value they bring. We empower everyone to have autonomy in their role and encourage them to use their voice in how we can make a positive impact as a firm and shape the future of real estate, the built environment and the impact on the communities we serve. Our collaborative and engaged teams provide excellent and dedicated client service. In our workplace, opinions are respected, everyone is invited to contribute to the success of our business and innovation, and new ideas are celebrated. In 2021 we celebrated our 125th anniversary and set out Our Desired Future, cemented in three pillars: People Potential, Client Centricity and always Creating our Future, together. Learn more about what sets us apart here. **About The Role** We are growing our market‑leading Valuation \& Advisory division and are seeking ambitious Chartered Valuation Surveyors at all levels — from graduate surveyor/newly qualified surveyors ready to build their expertise, to senior leaders seeking a platform to develop and mentor teams and drive client relationships. Apply now, if you want to work with a firm that values technical excellence, supports professional growth, and gives you exposure to some of Ireland’s most significant assets and clients. **Key Responsibilities** ***Newly Qualified Surveyors/Graduates*** * Assist in preparing high\-quality RICS Red Book valuation and advisory reports for banks, funds, developers, and corporate clients * Conduct inspections, market research, and comparable analysis * Support senior colleagues on complex instructions * Build strong client relationships and develop commercial awareness * Contribute to pitches, proposals, and team initiatives ***Senior up to Director\-Level Surveyors*** * Lead valuation instructions and manage key client relationships * Oversee and review valuation reports to ensure accuracy and compliance * Engage in Landlord \& Tenant matters, including rent reviews and lease renewals. * Contribute to the periodic review of team processes and procedures. * Mentor and support junior surveyors * Provide expert advice on complex assets, portfolios, and development appraisals * Contribute to shared knowledge within the team and the wider group. * Generate and win new business through networking and client engagement. **Requirements** * MRICS/MSCSI \- VRS Registered Valuer * Strong Technical Knowledge of valuation methodologies including Discounted Cash Flows. * Knowledge of Argus Enterprise and Developer Valuation packages. * Professional presentation with good customer service and communication skills required. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills \& attention to detail is essential. * Strong time management and organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. * Team player with strong interpersonal skills Competitive salary Please note: this is a Direct Search led by Knight Frank. Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs, even if provided by PSL agencies.

title: Veterinary Nurse - Graduate / Junior Position
description: **Cherrywood Veterinary Clinic, Dublin Full\-time, Permanent Salary €30,000\-€32,000 DOE Start date: May 2026 (flexible)** **A Supportive Start to Your Nursing Career** Starting your career as a Veterinary Nurse is a big step, and finding the right environment makes all the difference. At Cherrywood Veterinary Clinic, we offer a genuinely supportive, close\-knit team where you’ll be guided, encouraged and given the time to grow in confidence. This is a fantastic opportunity for a graduate or junior nurse to join a busy small animal clinic that combines excellent general practice with specialist endoscopy services. You’ll gain exposure to a varied caseload while building strong, practical skills in a setting that never feels overwhelming. **Learn in a Small, Collaborative Team** You’ll be working as part of a small, friendly team made up of two vets, two nurses and two animal care assistants. As one half of a two\-nurse team, you’ll play a key role in the day\-to\-day running of the clinic while always having support close by. This environment allows you to get properly involved, ask questions, and learn by doing. You’ll be supported in developing your skills across all areas of nursing, with particular opportunities to get involved in surgical work and endoscopy as your confidence grows. **A Unique Clinical Environment** Cherrywood Veterinary Clinic is a modern, 100% small animal practice with a special interest in endoscopy. This gives you the chance to be part of more advanced procedures while still building strong foundations in general practice nursing. The clinic is ideally located just a short walk from Cherrywood Luas and close to Loughlinstown Hospital, making it easily accessible while being part of a great local community. **A Working Pattern That Supports You** This is a full\-time role based on a 40\-hour week, with no weekends, no out\-of\-hours and no bank holiday requirements. It’s a rare opportunity to achieve a genuinely healthy work\-life balance from the very start of your career, while still gaining valuable clinical experience. **Who This Role Is For** This position is open to graduate or junior nurses, and prior experience is not essential. What matters most is your attitude and willingness to learn. You’ll need to be VCI registered, or due to join the register in 2026\. Beyond that, we’re looking for someone who is motivated, proactive, and takes pride in delivering high\-quality patient care. If you enjoy being part of a team, show initiative in your work, and want to build a strong foundation for your future career, you’ll fit in well here. **Ongoing Support and Development** We understand how important those first few years in practice are, which is why you’ll have access to structured support through our Graduate Nurse Academy. This includes mentoring, learning resources and guidance to help you develop at the right pace. You’ll also have access to funded CPD, further training opportunities, and the option to pursue additional qualifications as your interests develop. **Benefits \- Subject To Pro Rata** We recognise that our people are fundamental to the success of our business. Investing in our staff, premises and processes is at the heart of what we do. In addition to a competitive salary, you will also benefit from: **Work life balance** * 22 days annual leave entitlement * Birthday leave **Focus on employee wellbeing** * Paid Maternity, Paternity and Adoptive leave packages * Cycle to work scheme * EAP and wellbeing centre access * Paid bereavement leave * Company sick pay **Development** * €600 annual CPD allowance with 3 days paid (pro\-rata) * Bespoke Graduate Nurse Academy, Nurse Evolve and many more in\-house academies * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Leadership Academy for all future leaders, including Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Career progression opportunities **Professional memberships** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid **Additional Benefits** * Annual pay reviews * Pawesome benefits \- various discounts including retails and virtual gym * Employee pension contributions * Discounted staff pet veterinary fees * Relocation packages * Free flu jabs * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQIA\+, Wellbeing Champions, Nurse Committee, Clinical Board and many more) **A Place to Grow** Cherrywood Veterinary Clinic offers more than just your first job. It’s a place where you’ll feel supported, valued and part of a team from day one. If you’re looking for a positive, welcoming environment where you can build confidence, develop your skills and enjoy coming to work, we’d love to hear from you. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Up to €32,000 DOE

title: Veterinary Surgeon – Small Animal (GP Focus)
description: **Veterinary Surgeon – Small Animal (GP Focus)** ------------------------------------------------ **Vetcare Athy, Co. Kildare** **Full\-time, Fixed Term Contract (Part\-time considered)** **Salary up to €70,000 DOE (FTE)** **Start date from May 2026 (flexible)** -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- **A Solid GP Role in a Well\-Run, Supportive Practice** ------------------------------------------------------- Vetcare Athy is seeking a confident, well\-rounded small animal Veterinary Surgeon to join the team on a maternity cover contract. This role is ideal for an experienced GP vet who enjoys the rhythm of first opinion practice, is comfortable managing a varied caseload, and takes pride in delivering consistent, high\-quality care. You’ll be joining a busy but well\-organised clinic where the focus is on doing the fundamentals well. This is a role for someone who values good clinical standards, enjoys building client relationships, and is confident across routine consults, surgery and day\-to\-day case management. **The Role – Confident First Opinion Practice** ----------------------------------------------- This is a full\-time position working 42 hours per week, Monday to Friday from 9:00am to 6:00pm, with a 1 in 4 Saturday rota from 9:00am to 3:00pm. You’ll be managing a broad and steady caseload, including consultations, routine surgeries, dentistry and medical work\-ups. The role will suit someone who is comfortable with the pace of general practice and capable of working independently when needed, including periods of sole charge. There are no out\-of\-hours requirements, no Sundays and no bank holiday work, giving you a predictable schedule and a good balance outside of work. **A Practice That Supports Good Clinical Work** ----------------------------------------------- Vetcare Athy is the flagship clinic within the Vetcare group, offering a well\-equipped and professionally run environment. While the focus remains on first opinion care, the practice also supports more complex case work\-ups and procedures, giving you the option to expand your clinical exposure if that’s of interest. Facilities include digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound and a fully equipped in\-house laboratory, allowing you to work cases to a high standard. The team is experienced, approachable and collaborative, creating an environment where you can focus on doing your job well without unnecessary pressure. **Flexible Options to Suit You** -------------------------------- While this is advertised as a full\-time position, we are very open to hearing from experienced vets seeking part\-time or alternative working patterns. If you’re looking for something that better fits around other commitments, let us know what works for you and we’ll do our best to accommodate where possible. **Who This Role Will Suit** --------------------------- This position is best suited to an experienced Veterinary Surgeon who is confident in routine small animal practice. You’ll be comfortable with consultations, diagnostics, routine soft tissue surgery and dentistry, and happy managing cases from start to finish. You’ll also be someone who works well within a team, communicates clearly, and takes a practical, steady approach to clinical work. This is not a role for a new graduate, but it is ideal for a GP vet who enjoys the variety and consistency of first opinion practice and wants to work in a well\-supported setting. **Part of a Wider, Supportive Network** --------------------------------------- As part of the Vetcare group and IVC Evidensia, you’ll have access to a wider clinical network, resources and potential future opportunities. While this is a fixed\-term contract, it can be a great way to build relationships within the group and explore longer\-term options if they arise. **A Package That Reflects Your Experience** ------------------------------------------- Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll receive a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your wellbeing and professional development. This includes generous annual leave, paid CPD, professional memberships, wellbeing supports, pension contributions and a range of additional benefits. **A Straightforward, High\-Quality GP Role** -------------------------------------------- If you’re looking for a role where you can step in, feel confident in your work, and be part of a supportive, well\-run practice, this could be an excellent fit. We’d love to hear from you. Up to €70,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Waiter/Waitress
description: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. Benefits: * Free Food on shift * Plus Tips \- You'll receive tips/Tronc\* * 50% off food and drink when you’re not working * Wellbeing Support for you and your family * Referral Bonus of €450 for referring pizza chefs and €1150 for referring managers * Pension \& Life Assurance * Pay Rate is €13 for under 20s and €14\.15 for age 20 and over * Learning and Development Opportunities to invest in you You’ll be dedicated to delivering top\-notch service creating enlivening experiences that exceed expectations. Be yourself, bring your personality, and let every day be an opportunity to express your unique flair. No experience required. What you’ll do: * Greet and seat customers * Take orders and serve food, drink and desserts * Work in a dynamic and energetic environment Who we are: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. We’ve been around since 1965, bringing pizza to the UK and have since grown to a multichannel, international brand. Our people are at the heart of our success and everything we do; we believe you should always be able to express yourself. Here you can be yourself. Inclusivity and respect are at the heart of our business and are valued by our teams, customers, and suppliers. PizzaExpress and Milano is a place where everyone is welcome, and everyone belongs. That’s why we assess each application on the aptitude to do the job and nothing else. If you are invited to an interview, please let your interviewer know at the time of booking if you require any reasonable adjustments to be made for you. * Tronc, or card gratuities, is a fair way to pay gratuities to our teams. All Tronc goes to you, shared between our waiting and our non\-waiting teams. You’ll get this at the end of each pay period, along with your normal pay and it’s one of the best perks about working in hospitality.

title: Wildlife and Habitat Conservation Scientist
description: **Wildlife and Habitat Conservation Scientist (AI Training)** **About The Role** We’re seeking wildlife and habitat conservation scientists to help evaluate AI systems trained on biodiversity protection and ecosystem management. * Organization: Alignerr (Powered by Labelbox) * Location: Remote * Type: Hourly / Task\-based Contract * Compensation: $30\-55 /hour * Commitment: 10\-40 hours/week **What You’ll Do** * Review wildlife and habitat conservation scenarios used in AI training. * Assess accuracy of ecological reasoning and conservation strategies. * Identify unrealistic assumptions or misapplied conservation methods. * Provide structured feedback to improve ecological validity. * Work independently and asynchronously to meet project deadlines. **Who You Are** * 3\+ years of experience in wildlife biology, ecology, or habitat conservation. * Strong understanding of biodiversity and ecosystem management. * Able to evaluate applied ecological reasoning in written form. * Comfortable reviewing structured scientific content. **Nice to Have** * Graduate degree in Ecology, Wildlife Biology, or related field. * Field research or conservation program experience. * Familiarity with AI or content evaluation workflows. **Why Join Us** * Competitive pay and flexible remote work. * Lead a team working on cutting\-edge AI projects. * Exposure to advanced LLMs and how they’re trained. * Freelance perks: autonomy, flexibility, and global collaboration. * Potential for contract extension. **Application Process (Takes 10\-15 min)** * Submit your resume * Complete a short screening * Project matching and onboarding *PS: Our team reviews applications daily. Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.*
